data.narrative:
Picogrid is a Series A-stage company in Defense & Security that has raised $45M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms, including same-stage peers Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Ares Industries ($150M), and NODA AI ($125M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $45M total raised as a baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implying a $90M valuation. Peer-Median analysis examines the 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables; their median valuation is $225M, with a range spanning $125M to $500M. This reflects material dispersion in how the market values early-stage defense-tech companies.

The triangulated range across both methods yields a low of $76M, median of $158M, and high of $259M. The wide spread between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects genuine uncertainty: it is unclear whether Picogrid's capital efficiency and market position align with the lower quartile or median of its peer cohort. Key uncertainties include customer concentration, IP defensibility, and execution risk relative to better-capitalized competitors. A valuation in the $125M–$200M range appears most defensible given the data, though validation against contract pipeline and technical differentiation is warranted.
